description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped cluster of gray stone blocks, with rough, jagged surfaces and sharp edges. The most prominent feature is a red pictograph resembling a horse or similar animal, painted on the largest, flattest face of the central stone. The stones are stacked in a loosely cohesive pile, with some smaller fragments and rubble at the base, suggesting a natural or minimally altered formation. The surface texture is coarse and uneven, with visible cracks, chips, and angular protrusions. The coloration varies from medium to light gray, with some areas appearing almost white due to dust or weathering. The overall appearance suggests a weathered, ancient stone, possibly used as a marker or monument in an urban park or historical site.
description: A cluster of rough gray stones with a red animal pictograph painted on the largest face, featuring jagged edges and a weathered, uneven surface.
description_view_0: The front view shows the red animal pictograph clearly on the largest stone face, with several angular stones stacked around it.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the elongated, sloped surface of the main stone, with less visibility of the pictograph and more focus on the stone's layered structure.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a mostly pale, dust-covered surface with minimal visible markings and a smoother, more uniform appearance.
description_view_3: This angled view partially shows the red pictograph and emphasizes the jagged, broken edges and the depth of the stone cluster.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Features a hand-painted red animal pictograph on one face, with natural veining and irregular cracks throughout the stone surfaces.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible chips, cracks, and some dust accumulation indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban environments but may blend with natural landscaping or historical installations; sits atop the ground with some loose rubble at the base.
